title: Relax on Baker Beach
content: #21 in Best Things To Do in San Francisco. While San Francisco isn't known for being a beach town, the city's Baker Beach is often considered one of the best in California. Located in the northwestern area of San Francisco in the Presidio, Baker Beach is primarily known for its sweeping views of the Golden Gate Bridge and the neighboring Marin Headlands. Travelers flock here to snap photos of the bridge, however, if you're looking to catch some rays or get your feet wet, you should go elsewhere: Swimming at Baker Beach is dangerous due to large waves, undertow and rip currents, and the city's fickle weather means it's not always warm enough for sunbathing. And those traveling with families will want to stick to the south side of the beach: North Baker Beach is clothing optional, and it's definitely practiced. The beach is open all hours of the day, though considering the dangerous tide, it's not recommended to visit at night.

content: I would recommend attending this beach in a group if you are a woman. There is alot of nude males who tend to make things uncomfortable for people on the beach.

title: Baker Beach, San Francisco, California, United States — Beach Review | Condé Nast Traveler
content: # Baker Beach. Baker Beach is a local San Francisco favorite, especially on warm days—which are rare. But even when Karl the Fog settles in, the soft sand, Pacific Ocean sounds, and views make it a worthwhile visit. San Francisco weather is cool, especially on the beach, so dress in layers. Since picnicking is allowed on Baker Beach, feel free to bring a stash of local goodies, but be sure and pack up the trash when you leave. Hikers should check out the north end of Baker Beach, where the Sand Ladder connects to the California Coastal Trail and the Batteries to Bluffs Trail. title: BAKER BEACH - San Francisco, California - Yelp
content: This is my go-to beach whenever I'm in SF. So pretty and plenty of space for a nice picnic/beach day. We went on St.Patty's weekend so it was busier than usual
